name: calendra

metadata.name: calendra
metadata.summary: Worldwide holidays and working days helper and toolkit.

documentation:
[![](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/calendra.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/calendra)

![](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/calendra.svg)

[![tests](https://github.com/jaraco/calendra/actions/workflows/main.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/jaraco/calendra/actions?query=workflow%3A%22tests%22)

[![Ruff](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/charliermarsh/ruff/main/assets/badge/v2.json)](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff)

[![](https://readthedocs.org/projects/calendra/badge/?version=latest)](https://calendra.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)

[![](https://img.shields.io/badge/skeleton-2024-informational)](https://blog.jaraco.com/skeleton)


## Overview

Calendra is a Python module that offers classes able to handle calendars, list legal / religious holidays and gives working-day-related computation functions.

## History

Calendra is a fork of [Workalendar](https://github.com/workalendar/workalendar)
designed to be more extensible and introspectable, adding interfaces where
[Workalendar is philosophically opposed for the sake of simplicity](https://github.com/workalendar/workalendar/pull/79).

What can Calendra do that Workalendar cannot?

- Provides descriptions for holidays for the "day indicated" for each
  Holiday (such as '3rd Monday in August').
- Keeps distinct the indicated and observed dates for Holidays, such
  that it's possible to determine on what day a given holiday is observed.
- Allows the number of Holidays in a calendar year to be counted.
- Consolidates observance logic in the core code rather than requiring
  each calendar implementation to implement its own.

## Status

The project is stable and in production use. Calendra follows the principles of [semver](https://semver.org) for released verisons.

If you spot any bug or wish to add a calendar, please refer to the [Contributing doc](https://peopledoc.github.io/workalendar/contributing.html).

## Usage sample

```python-repl
>>> from datetime import date
>>> from calendra.europe import France
>>> cal = France()
>>> cal.holidays(2012)
[(datetime.date(2012, 1, 1), 'New year'),
 (datetime.date(2012, 4, 9), 'Easter Monday'),
 (datetime.date(2012, 5, 1), 'Labour Day'),
 (datetime.date(2012, 5, 8), 'Victory in Europe Day'),
 (datetime.date(2012, 5, 17), 'Ascension Day'),
 (datetime.date(2012, 5, 28), 'Whit Monday'),
 (datetime.date(2012, 7, 14), 'Bastille Day'),
 (datetime.date(2012, 8, 15), 'Assumption of Mary to Heaven'),
 (datetime.date(2012, 11, 1), "All Saints' Day"),
 (datetime.date(2012, 11, 11), 'Armistice Day'),
 (datetime.date(2012, 12, 25), 'Christmas')]
>>> cal.is_working_day(date(2012, 12, 25))  # it's Christmas
False
>>> cal.is_working_day(date(2012, 12, 30))  # it's Sunday
False
>>> cal.is_working_day(date(2012, 12, 26))
True
>>> cal.add_working_days(date(2012, 12, 23), 5)  # 5 working days after Xmas
datetime.date(2012, 12, 31)
```

For a more complete documentation and advanced usage, go to [the official workalendar documentation](https://workalendar.github.io/workalendar).

## External dependencies

Calendra has been tested on the Python versions declared in setup.cfg.

## Tests

To run test, just install tox with `pip install tox` and run `tox`
from the command line.


## Available Calendars

### Europe

- Austria
- Belarus
- Belgium
- Bulgaria
- Cayman Islands
- Croatia
- Cyprus
- Czech Republic
- Denmark
- Estonia
- European Central Bank
- Finland
- France
- France (Alsace / Moselle)
- Georgia
- Germany
- Greece
- Guernsey
- Hungary
- Iceland
- Ireland
- Italy
- Latvia
- Lithuania
- Luxembourg
- Malta
- Monaco
- Netherlands (optionally with school holidays and carnival)
- Norway
- Poland
- Portugal
- Romania
- Russia
- Serbia
- Slovakia
- Slovenia
- Spain (Andalusia, Aragon, Castile and León, Castilla-La Mancha, Canary Islands, Extremadura, Galicia, Balearic Islands, La Rioja, Community of Madrid, Murcia, Navarre, Asturias, Basque Country, Cantabria, Valencian Community)
- Sweden
- Switzerland (Aargau, Appenzell Innerrhoden, Appenzell Ausserrhoden, Bern, Basel-Landschaft, Basel-Stadt, Fribourg, Geneva, Glarus, Graubünden, Jura, Luzern, Neuchâtel, Nidwalden, Obwalden, St. Gallen, Schaffhausen, Solothurn, Schwyz, Thurgau, Ticino, Uri, Vaud, Valais, Zug, Zurich)
- Turkey
- Ukraine
- United Kingdom (incl. Northern Ireland, Scotland and all its territories)

### America

- Argentina
- Barbados
- Brazil (all states, cities and for bank transactions, except the city of Viana)
- Canada (including provincial and territory holidays)
- Chile
- Colombia
- El Salvador
- Mexico
- Panama
- Paraguay
- United States of America
  - State holidays for all the 50 States
  - American Samoa
  - Chicago, Illinois
  - Guam
  - Suffolk County, Massachusetts
  - California Education, Berkeley, San Francisco, West Hollywood
  - Florida Legal and Florida Circuit Courts, Miami-Dade
  - Federal Reserve System

### Asia

- China
- Hong Kong
- Israel
- Japan
- JapanBank
- Kazakhstan
- Malaysia
- Philippines
- Qatar
- Singapore
- South Korea
- Taiwan

### Oceania

- Australia (incl. its different states)
- Marshall Islands
- New Zealand

### Africa

- Algeria
- Angola
- Benin
- Ivory Coast
- Kenya
- Madagascar
- Mozambique
- Nigeria
- São Tomé
- South Africa
- Tunisia

And more to come (I hope!)

## Caveats

Please take note that some calendars are not 100% accurate. The most common example is the Islamic calendar, where some computed holidays are not exactly on the same official day decided by religious authorities, and this may vary country by country. Whenever it's possible, try to adjust your results with the official data provided by the adequate authorities.

Some countries have some holidays based on ephemerids and equinoxes. Those are computed for the previous and next 30 years to prevent big computations and dependencies.

## Contributing

Please read our [contributing.md](https://github.com/workalendar/workalendar/blob/master/docs/contributing.md) document to discover how you can contribute to `workalendar`. Pull-requests are very welcome.

## History

This project was born in 2013, as an answer to the question "how do we calculate *this date + 5 working days*?". It was a use-case for [PeopleDoc](https://www.people-doc.fr/), in their ticketing application, for calculating SLAs. And it began as a small open-source library, for France and the USA at first.

Thanks to PeopleDoc, this project grew and eventually became a world-wide library, with the help of dozens of contributors.

As of June 2021, this project has moved to its own organization.

## License

This library is published under the terms of the MIT License. Please check the LICENSE file for more details.

modules.name: calendra.usa.virginia
documentation:
Virginia module

You may or may want not to treat the Day before Thanksgiving as a non-working
day by implementing the following class:

```
from workalenda.usa import Virginia as VirginiaBase

class Virginia(VirginiaBase):
    include_thanksgiving_wednesday = False
```

modules.name: calendra.usa.west_virginia
documentation:
West Virginia

Christmas Eve and New Years Eve are considered as half-holidays. By default,
they're not included as "non-working days". If for your personal use you want
to include them, you may just have to create a class like this:

```
class WestVirginiaIncludeEves(WestVirginia):
    west_virginia_include_christmas_eve = True
    west_virginia_include_nye = True
```

modules.name: calendra.usa.texas
documentation:
# Texas module

This module presents two classes to handle the way state holidays are managed
in Texas.

The {class}`TexasBase` class gathers all available holidays for Texas,
according to this document:
<http://www.statutes.legis.state.tx.us/Docs/GV/htm/GV.662.htm>

The {class}`Texas` class includes all national and state holidays, as described
in the said document. This should be the "default" Texas calendar class, to be
used in most cases.

But if state holidays are supposed to be observed by most of the workforces,
any employee can chose to skip one of these days and replace it by another.

If at some point you need to create a specific calendar class based on Texas
calendar, you can either use the {class}`TexasBase` class or directly the
{class}`Texas` class and overwrite/override the {method}`get_fixed_holidays()`
and/or {method}`get_variable_days()` to fit your needs.

Example:

```
class TexasCustom(TexasBase):
    # This will include the confederate heroes day
    texas_include_confederate_heroes = True

    FIXED_HOLIDAYS = TexasBase.FIXED_HOLIDAYS + (
        (7, 14, "Bastille Day!"),
    )

    def get_variable_days(self, year):
        days = super().get_variable_days(year)
        days.append(
            (self.get_nth_weekday_in_month(year, 1, 15), "Special Day")
        )
        return days
```

modules.name: calendra.skyfield_astronomy
documentation: Astronomical functions

functions.name: calendra.skyfield_astronomy.calculate_equinoxes
documentation: calculate equinox with time zone

functions.name: calendra.skyfield_astronomy.get_current_longitude
documentation: Return the ecliptic longitude, in radians.

functions.name: calendra.skyfield_astronomy.newton
documentation:
Return an x-value at which the given function reaches zero.

Stops and declares victory once the x-value is within `precision`
of the solution, which defaults to a half-second of clock time.

functions.name: calendra.skyfield_astronomy.newton_angle_function
documentation:
Compute the longitude of body2 relative to body1

In our case, it's Earth & Sun, but it could be used as any other
combination of solar system planets/bodies.

functions.name: calendra.skyfield_astronomy.solar_term
documentation:
Returns the date of the solar term for the given longitude
and the given year.

Solar terms are used for Chinese and Taiwanese holidays
(e.g. Qingming Festival in Taiwan).

More information:
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_term
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Qingming

This function is adapted from the following topic:
https://answers.launchpad.net/pyephem/+question/110832

methods.name: calendra.registry.IsoRegistry.register
documentation: Store the `cls` in the region_registry.

methods.name: calendra.registry.IsoRegistry.load_module_from_items
documentation: Load all registered classes in the registry

methods.name: calendra.registry.IsoRegistry.get
documentation:
Retrieve calendar class associated with given `iso_code`.

If calendar of subdivision is not registered
(for subdivision like ISO codes, e.g. GB-ENG)
returns calendar of containing region
(e.g. United Kingdom for ISO code GB) if it's available.

```
rtype

Calendar
```

methods.name: calendra.registry.IsoRegistry.get_subregions
documentation:
Returns subregion calendar classes for given region iso_code.

```pycon
>>> registry = IsoRegistry()
>>> # assuming calendars registered are: DE, DE-HH, DE-BE
>>> registry.get_subregions('DE')  # doctest: +SKIP
{'DE-HH': <class 'calendra.europe.germany.Hamburg'>,
'DE-BE': <class 'calendra.europe.germany.Berlin'>}
:rtype dict
:return dict where keys are ISO codes strings
and values are calendar classes
```

methods.name: calendra.registry.IsoRegistry.get_calendars
documentation:
Returns calendar classes for regions

:param region_codes list of ISO codes for selected regions. If empty,
                    the function will return all items from the
                    registry.
:param include_subregions boolean if subregions
of selected regions should be included in result
:rtype dict
:return dict where keys are ISO codes strings
and values are calendar classes

Registry for all calendars retrievable
by ISO 3166-2 codes associated with countries
where they are used as official calendars.

Two letter codes are favored for any subdivisions.

functions.name: calendra.tests.test_skyfield_astronomy.params_newton_angle
documentation: Session-scoped fixture to "cache" the newton angle func parameters

functions.name: calendra.tests.test_skyfield_astronomy.test_newton_angle_function_normal_range
documentation: Test the newton angle func when the longitude is in the range [-pi, +pi].

functions.name: calendra.tests.test_skyfield_astronomy.test_newton_angle_function_above_pi
documentation:
Test the newton angle function when the longitude is > +pi.

This should not happen, but it was implemented in the function, so in order
to make sure that the resulting angle is always in the range [-pi, +pi],
we added these tests with those out of range values.

functions.name: calendra.tests.test_skyfield_astronomy.test_newton_angle_function_below_minus_pi
documentation:
Test the newton angle function when the longitude is < -pi.

This should not happen, but it was implemented in the function, so in order
to make sure that the resulting angle is always in the range [-pi, +pi],
we added these tests with those out of range values.

functions.name: calendra.tests.test_astronomy.test_newton_angle_function_above_pi
documentation:
Test the newton angle function when the longitude is > +pi.

This should not happen, but it was implemented in the function, so in order
to make sure that the resulting angle is always in the range [-pi, +pi],
we added these tests with those out of range values.

functions.name: calendra.tests.test_astronomy.test_newton_angle_function_below_minus_pi

Test the newton angle function when the longitude is < -pi.

This should not happen, but it was implemented in the function, so in order
to make sure that the resulting angle is always in the range [-pi, +pi],
we added these tests with those out of range values.

modules.name: calendra.registry_tools
documentation: Tools to update the ISO registry.

functions.name: calendra.registry_tools.iso_register
documentation:
Registers Calendar class as country or region in IsoRegistry.

Registered country must set class variables `iso` using this decorator.

```pycon
>>> from calendra.core import Calendar
>>> from calendra.registry import registry
>>> from calendra.registry_tools import iso_register
>>> @iso_register('MC-MR')
... class MyRegion(Calendar):
...     'My Region'
```

Region calendar is then retrievable from registry:

```pycon
>>> calendar = registry.get('MC-MR')
```

methods.name: calendra.holiday.Holiday.get_observed_date
documentation:
The date the holiday is observed for the calendar. If the holiday
occurs on a weekend, it may be observed on another day as indicated by
the observance_shift.

The holiday may also specify an 'observe_after' such that it is always
shifted after a preceding holiday. For example, Boxing day is always
observed after Christmas Day is observed.

A named holiday with an indicated date, name, and additional keyword
attributes.

```pycon
>>> nyd = Holiday(date(2014, 1, 1), "New year")
```

But if New Year's Eve is also a holiday, and it too falls on a weekend,
many calendars will have that holiday fall back to the previous friday:

```pycon
>>> from dateutil import relativedelta as rd
>>> nye = Holiday(date(2014, 12, 31), "New year's eve",
...     observance_shift=dict(weekday=rd.FR(-1)))
```

For compatibility, a Holiday may be treated like a tuple of (date, label)

```pycon
>>> nyd[0] == date(2014, 1, 1)
True
>>> nyd[1]
'New year'
>>> d, label = nyd
```

classes.name: calendra.core.ChineseNewYearMixin

methods.name: calendra.core.ChineseNewYearMixin.get_chinese_new_year
documentation:
Compute Chinese New Year days. To return a list of holidays.

By default, it'll at least return the Chinese New Year holidays chosen
using the following options:

- `include_chinese_new_year_eve`
- `include_chinese_new_year` (on by default)
- `include_chinese_second_day`

If the `shift_sunday_holidays` option is on, the rules are the
following.

- If the CNY1 falls on MON-FRI, there's not shift.
- If the CNY1 falls on SAT, the CNY2 is shifted to the Monday after.
- If the CNY1 falls on SUN, the CNY1 is shifted to the Monday after,
  and CNY2 is shifted to the Tuesday after.

methods.name: calendra.core.ChineseNewYearMixin.observance_shift
documentation: Taking an existing holiday, return a 'shifted' day to skip on from SUN.
Calendar including toolsets to compute the Chinese New Year holidays.

methods.name: calendra.core.CalverterMixin.get_delta_islamic_holidays
documentation:
Return the delta to add/substract according to the year or customs.

By default, to return None or timedelta(days=0)

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.get_fixed_holidays
documentation: Return the fixed days according to the FIXED_HOLIDAYS class property

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.get_calendar_holidays
documentation:
Get calendar holidays.
If you want to override this, please make sure that it **must** return
a list of tuples (date, holiday_name).

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.holidays
documentation:
Computes holidays (non-working days) for a given year.
Return a 2-item tuple, composed of the date and a label.

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.get_holiday_label
documentation: Return the label of the holiday, if the date is a holiday

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.get_weekend_days
documentation:
Return a list (or a tuple) of weekdays that are *not* working days.

e.g: return (SAT, SUN,)

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.is_working_day
documentation:
Return True if it's a working day.
In addition to the regular holidays, you can add exceptions.

By providing `extra_working_days`, you'll state that these dates
**are** working days.

By providing `extra_holidays`, you'll state that these dates **are**
holidays, even if not in the regular calendar holidays (or weekends).

Please note that the `extra_working_days` list has priority over the
`extra_holidays` list.

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.is_holiday
documentation:
Return True if it's an holiday.
In addition to the regular holidays, you can add exceptions.

By providing `extra_holidays`, you'll state that these dates **are**
holidays, even if not in the regular calendar holidays (or weekends).

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.is_observed_holiday
documentation: Return True if it's an observed holiday.

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.add_working_days
documentation:
Add `delta` working days to the date.

You can provide either a date or a datetime to this function that will
output a `date` result. You can alter this behaviour using the
`keep_datetime` option set to `True`.

the `delta` parameter might be positive or negative. If it's
negative, you may want to use the `sub_working_days()` method with
a positive `delta` argument.

By providing `extra_working_days`, you'll state that these dates
**are** working days.

By providing `extra_holidays`, you'll state that these dates **are**
holidays, even if not in the regular calendar holidays (or weekends).

Please note that the `extra_working_days` list has priority over the
`extra_holidays` list.

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.sub_working_days
documentation:
Substract `delta` working days to the date.

This method is a shortcut / helper. Users may want to use either:

```
cal.add_working_days(my_date, -7)
cal.sub_working_days(my_date, 7)
```

The other parameters are to be used exactly as in the
`add_working_days` method.

A negative `delta` argument will be converted into its absolute
value. Hence, the two following calls are equivalent:

```
cal.sub_working_days(my_date, -7)
cal.sub_working_days(my_date, 7)
```

As in `add_working_days()` you can set the parameter
`keep_datetime` to `True` to make sure that if your `day`
argument is a `datetime`, the returned date will also be a
`datetime` object.

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.find_following_working_day
documentation:
Looks for the following working day, if not already a working day.

**WARNING**: this function doesn't take into account the calendar
holidays, only the days of the week and the weekend days parameters.

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.get_nth_weekday_in_month
documentation:
Get the nth weekday in a given month. e.g:

```pycon
>>> # the 1st monday in Jan 2013
>>> Calendar.get_nth_weekday_in_month(2013, 1, MON)
datetime.date(2013, 1, 7)
>>> # The 2nd monday in Jan 2013
>>> Calendar.get_nth_weekday_in_month(2013, 1, MON, 2)
datetime.date(2013, 1, 14)
```

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.get_last_weekday_in_month
documentation:
Get the last weekday in a given month. e.g:

```pycon
>>> # the last monday in Jan 2013
>>> Calendar.get_last_weekday_in_month(2013, 1, MON)
datetime.date(2013, 1, 28)
```

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.get_iso_week_date
documentation:
Return the date of the weekday of the week number (ISO definition).

**Warning:** in the ISO definition, the weeks start on MON, not SUN.

By default, if you don't provide the `weekday` argument, it'll return
the date of the MON of this week number.

Example:

> ```pycon
> >>> Calendar.get_iso_week_date(2021, 44)
> datetime.date(2021, 11, 1)
> ```

For your convenience, the ISO weekdays are available via the
`calendra.core` module, like this:

> from calendra.core import ISO_MON, ISO_TUE # etc.

i.e.: if you need to get the FRI of the week 44 of the year 2020,
you'll have to use:

> from calendra.core import ISO_FRI
> Calendar.get_iso_week_date(2020, 44, ISO_FRI)

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.get_first_weekday_after
documentation:
Get the first weekday after a given day. If the day is the same
weekday, the same day will be returned.

```pycon
>>> # the first monday after Apr 1 2015
>>> Calendar.get_first_weekday_after(date(2015, 4, 1), MON)
datetime.date(2015, 4, 6)
```

```pycon
>>> # the first tuesday after Apr 14 2015
>>> Calendar.get_first_weekday_after(date(2015, 4, 14), TUE)
datetime.date(2015, 4, 14)
```

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.get_working_days_delta
documentation:
Return the number of working day between two given dates.
The order of the dates provided doesn't matter.

In the following example, there are 5 days, because of the week-end:

```pycon
>>> cal = WesternCalendar()  # does not include easter monday
>>> day1 = date(2018, 3, 29)
>>> day2 = date(2018, 4, 5)
>>> cal.get_working_days_delta(day1, day2)
5
```

In France, April 1st 2018 is a holiday because it's Easter monday:

```pycon
>>> from calendra.europe import France
>>> cal = France()
>>> cal.get_working_days_delta(day1, day2)
4
```

This method should even work if your `start` and `end` arguments
are datetimes.

By default, if the day after you start is not a working day,
the count will start at 0. If include_start is set to true,
this day will be taken into account.

Example:

```pycon
>>> from dateutil.parser import parse
>>> cal = France()
>>> day1 = parse('09/05/2018 00:01', dayfirst=True)
>>> day2 = parse('10/05/2018 19:01', dayfirst=True) # holiday in france
>>> cal.get_working_days_delta(day1, day2)
0
```

```pycon
>>> cal.get_working_days_delta(day1, day2, include_start=True)
1
```

As in many other methods, you can use the `extra_holidays` and
`extra_working_days` to exclude

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar._get_ical_period
documentation:
Return a usable period for iCal export

Default period is [2000, 2030]

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar._get_ical_target_path
documentation:
Return target path for iCal export.

# Note

If `target_path` does not have one of the extensions `.ical`, `.ics`,
`.ifb`, or `.icalendar`, the extension `.ics` is appended to the path.
Returns
\-------
None.
Examples
\--------
\>>> from calendra.europe import Austria
\>>> cal = Austria()
\>>> str(cal.\_get_ical_target_path('austria'))
'austria.ics'

methods.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.export_to_ical
documentation:
Export the calendar to iCal (RFC 5545) format.

Parameters
----------
period: [int, int]
    start and end year (inclusive) of calendar
    Default is [2000, 2030]

target_path: str or pathlib.Path
    the name or path of the exported file. If this argument is missing,
    the function will return the ical content.

class_variables.name: calendra.core.CoreCalendar.observance_shift

a rd.relativedelta instance.

methods.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.UnitedStatesTest.test_independence_day_nearest_weekday
documentation: Independence Day should shift to the nearest weekday.

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.NoColumbus
documentation:
Some States don't include Columbus Day:

* Alaska
* Arkansas
* California
* Delaware

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.NoPresidentialDay
documentation: Washington's birthday is not included in Delaware calendar.

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.InaugurationDay
documentation: When Inauguration Day is a public holiday

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.ElectionDayEvenYears
documentation: Some state include the election day on even years

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.ElectionDayPresidentialYears
documentation: Some state include the election day on presidential years

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.ElectionDayEveryYear
documentation: Some State include election day on every year

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.IncludeMardiGras
documentation: Louisiana and some areas (Alabama Counties) include Mardi Gras

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.CaliforniaBerkeleyTest
documentation: Like California, except that it has:
* No Chavez Day,
* Includes Columbus day, but relabels it.
* Adds Lincoln's Birthday.

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.CaliforniaSanFranciscoTest
documentation: Like California, except:
* No Chavez Day,
* Added Columbus Day

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.CaliforniaWestHollywoodTest
documentation: Like California, except:
* No Chavez Day,
* No Thanksgiving Friday
* Added Harvey Milk Day

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.FloridaBasicTest
documentation:
Core Florida tests.

The difference is that it includes the Thanksgiving Friday *and* its label
is renamed.

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.FloridaTest
documentation:
Florida includes all federal holidays except
Washington's Birthday & Columbus day

classes.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.FloridaLegalTest
documentation:
Florida Legal Holidays include:

* All Florida State Holidays,
* Mardi Gras,
* Lincoln's Birthday,
* Susan B. Anthony Day,
* Washington's Birthday,
* Good Friday,
* Pascua Florida Day,
* Confederate Memorial Day,
* Jefferson Davies Birthday,
* Flag Day
* Columbus Day renamed as "Columbus and Farmers' Day"
* Election Day

methods.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.IdahoTest.test_mlk_label
documentation: NOTE: Idaho only has federal holidays.

methods.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.SouthDakotaTest.test_columbus_day_label
documentation: NOTE: South Dakota has all federal holidays, except Columbus Day,
but it's renamed as "Native Americans Day"

methods.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.TexasBaseTest.test_state_year_2014
documentation: NOTE: "Stock" Texas doesn't include Columbus Day,
state holidays are handled differently

methods.name: calendra.tests.test_usa.WyomingTest.test_mlk_label
documentation: NOTE: Wyoming only has all federal holidays

Scotland calendar mixins.

There are so many of them that it became necessary to move them to a different
module.

documentation: National Heroes day: Fourth MON in January

methods.name: calendra.europe.cayman_islands.CaymanIslands.get_discovery_day

methods.name: calendra.europe.cayman_islands.CaymanIslands.get_queens_birthday

methods.name: calendra.europe.cayman_islands.CaymanIslands.get_constitution_day
documentation: Constitution Day: First MON of July.

methods.name: calendra.europe.cayman_islands.CaymanIslands.get_remembrance_day

modules.name: calendra.europe.scotland
documentation:
Scotland specific module.

The main source of information is:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_and_bank_holidays_in_Scotland

Note on "inheritance":

* Carnoustie and Monifieth area is a subdivision of Angus
* Lanark is part of South Lanarkshire

FIXME:

* Galashiels is part of the Scottish Borders
* Hawick is part of the Scottish Borders
* Kilmarnock is probably part of AyrShire (?)
* Lanark is part of South Lanarkshire
* Linlithgow... part of N/A
* Lochaber... part of N/A
* ...

methods.name: calendra.europe.scotland.Scotland.get_may_day
documentation: May Day is the first Monday in May

methods.name: calendra.europe.scotland.Scotland.get_spring_holiday
documentation:
Return spring holiday date and label.

You need to implement it as soon as the flag `include_spring_holiday`
is True.

methods.name: calendra.europe.scotland.Scotland.get_fair_holiday
documentation:
Return fair holiday date and label.

You need to implement it as soon as the flag `include_fair_holiday`
is True.

methods.name: calendra.europe.scotland.Scotland.get_autumn_holiday
documentation:
Return autumn holiday date and label.

You need to implement it as soon as the flag `include_autumn_holiday`
is True.

methods.name: calendra.europe.scotland.Scotland.get_victoria_day
documentation:
Return Victoria day date and label.

You need to implement it as soon as the flag `include_victoria_day`
is True.

methods.name: calendra.europe.scotland.Edinburgh.get_spring_holiday
documentation:
Return Spring Holiday for Edinburgh.

Set to the 3rd Monday of April, unless it falls on Easter Monday, then
it's shifted to previous week.

methods.name: calendra.europe.scotland.Edinburgh.get_victoria_day
documentation:
Return Victoria Day for Edinburgh.

Set to the Monday strictly before May 24th. It means that if May 24th
is a Monday, it's shifted to the week before.

methods.name: calendra.europe.scotland.Edinburgh.get_autumn_holiday
documentation:
Return Autumn Holiday for Edinburgh.

Set to the third Monday in September. Since it's the only region to
follow this rule, we won't have a Mixin associated to it.

classes.name: calendra.america.chile.Chile
documentation: https://www.feriados.cl
http://www.feriadoschilenos.cl

methods.name: calendra.america.paraguay.Paraguay.get_heroes_day
documentation:
Heroes Day is a fixed holidays.

In 2017, it has been moved to February 27th ; otherwise, it happens on
March 1st.

ref: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_holidays_in_Paraguay

methods.name: calendra.america.paraguay.Paraguay.get_founding_of_asuncion
documentation:
Return the Founding of Asunción.

In 2017, it has been moved to August 14th ; otherwise it happens on
August 15th.

methods.name: calendra.america.paraguay.Paraguay.get_boqueron_battle_victory_day
documentation:
Return Boqueron Battle Victory Day.

In 2017, it has been moved to October 2nd ; otherwise it happens on
September 29th.

methods.name: calendra.america.paraguay.Paraguay.get_fixed_holidays
documentation: Return fixed holidays for Paraguay.

methods.name: calendra.america.colombia.Colombia.get_epiphany
documentation: Epiphany is shifted in Colombia

methods.name: calendra.america.colombia.Colombia.get_day_of_the_races
documentation:
Return Day of the Races and Hispanity

a.k.a. "Día de la Raza"
Fixed to the next MON after October 12th (Columbus Day)

methods.name: calendra.america.colombia.Colombia.get_cartagena_independence
documentation:
Cartagena independance day

Fixed to the next MON after November 11th.

methods.name: calendra.america.colombia.Colombia.get_variable_days
documentation:
Return variable holidays for Colombia

The following days are set to "the next MON after the 'true' date".

* Epiphany,
* Saint Joseph,
* Ascension,
* Corpus Christi,
* Sacred Heart,
* Saint Peter & Saint Paul
* Assumption
* Columbus Day / Race Day
* All Saints
* Cartagena Independance

methods.name: calendra.america.barbados.Barbados.get_kadooment_day
documentation: First Monday of August.

methods.name: calendra.america.barbados.Barbados.get_variable_days
documentation: Return variable holidays of the Barbados calendar.

methods.name: calendra.america.barbados.Barbados.get_fixed_holidays
documentation:
Return fixed holidays of the Barbados calendar.

A shift day is appended if a fixed holiday happens on SUN.

methods.name: calendra.america.canada.BritishColumbia.get_family_day
documentation:
Return Family Day for British Columbia.

From 2013 to 2018, Family Day was on 2nd MON of February
As of 2019, Family Day happens on 3rd MON of February

methods.name: calendra.america.argentina.Argentina.get_general_guemes_day
documentation:
Día Paso a la Inmortalidad del General Martín Miguel de Güemes.

Happens on June 17th, except:

* if it happens on a THU, it's shifted to the next MON.
* if it happens on a WED, it's shifted to the MON before this date.

methods.name: calendra.america.argentina.Argentina.get_general_martin_day
documentation:
Día Paso a la Inmortalidad del Gral. José de San Martín

Third MON of August.

methods.name: calendra.america.argentina.Argentina.get_soberania_day
documentation:
Día de la Soberanía Nacional

Happens on the 3rd MON of November after the first Friday.

methods.name: calendra.america.argentina.Argentina.get_diversidad_day
documentation:
Día del Respeto a la Diversidad Cultural

The pivot date is the 12th of October.

* If it happens on a TUE, it's shifter on the 11th of Oct.
* If it happens on a WED, THU, FRI or SAT, it's shifted on the first
  MON after this date.
* Else, it's on the 12th of October.

methods.name: calendra.america.argentina.Argentina.get_malvinas_day
documentation:
Día de las Malvinas

In honour of the Veterans and the Fallen of the Malvinas war.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malvinas_Day

In 2020, it was shifted to March 31st because of
the coronavirus crisis.

methods.name: calendra.america.brazil.BrazilRioDeJaneiro.get_dia_do_comercio
documentation:
Return Dia do Comércio variable date

It happens on the 3rd Monday of october.

classes.name: calendra.america.brazil.BrazilBankCalendar

methods.name: calendra.america.brazil.BrazilBankCalendar.get_last_day_of_year_for_only_internal_bank_trans
documentation:
The last day of year isn't a working day for public bank
transactions in Brazil. More details can be read in
http://www.bcb.gov.br/pre/bc_atende/port/servicos4.asp

methods.name: calendra.america.brazil.BrazilBankCalendar.get_variable_days
documentation:
Define the brazilian variable holidays and the last
day for only internal bank transactions

methods.name: calendra.america.brazil.BrazilBankCalendar.find_following_working_day
documentation:
Find for the next working day by ignoring weekends,
fixed and non fixed holidays and the last working
day for only internal bank transactions in Brazil

Calendar that considers only working days for bank transactions
for companies and the general public
